bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, and the opponent’s opening offer gives me the minimum value on every issue. I am targeting maximum utility with a strong opening counteroffer, while staying within the valid domain.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early (t=0.02), so there is little need to concede much yet. The opponent has moved slightly toward my preferences on issueA, but their offers are still very low utility for me, so I will make only a small concession on my lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a very high utility because it is still the opening phase at t=0.04, so there is little reason to concede much yet. The opponent has only improved issueA from valueF to valueD while holding the other issues at valueF, so I will make a small concession on my least important issueD while holding firm on the higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.95 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is little reason to make large concessions yet. The opponent has conceded only on issueA while keeping issueB, issueC, and issueD at valueF, but since issueD is my lowest-weight issue, I can continue conceding there while holding firm on the more important issues.

I am still targeting a high utility bid around 0.94 because it is very early in the negotiation at t=0.08, so there is little need for major concessions yet. The opponent has conceded steadily on issueA but is holding issueB-D at valueF, so I will keep my highest-weight issues strong and make a small concession only on the lowest-weight issueD while avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession on the lowest-weight issue. The opponent has steadily conceded on issueA up to my best value but has held issueB, issueC, and issueD at valueF, so I will continue conceding only on issueD for now while protecting the more important issues.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.89 rather than making major concessions. The opponent has steadily improved issueA to my top value and only just started conceding issueB, while holding issueC and issueD at valueF; I will keep my high-weight issues A and B at valueA and make a small concession on lower-weight issueC while maintaining the prior concession on issueD.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.856 by holding firm on the most important issues, issueA and issueB, while conceding further on lower-weight issueC and already fully conceding issueD. Time is still early at t=0.14, so I do not need to rush to the opponent’s latest 0.616-utility offer, but their sequence shows steady concession from issueA into issueB, suggesting persistence may still pay off.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.82, since it is early in the negotiation and the opponent has been steadily improving their offers to me. Their bids show concessions first on issueA and now issueB, while they hold issueC and issueD at valueF, so I am conceding mainly on lower-weight issueC/issueD while protecting issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.79, which is a meaningful concession from my prior offer while still protecting my highest-weight issues. The opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me, especially on issueA and issueB, but they are still holding issueC and issueD at valueF; since it is still early at t=0.18, I will hold firm on issueA and issueB and concede mainly on lower-weight issueC/issueD.

I am targeting a utility a bit above 0.80 because it is still early at t=0.20, so I should not yet accept the opponent’s latest low-value concessions on issueC and issueD. The opponent has steadily conceded on issueA and issueB up to my top values, but has held firm at valueF on issueC and issueD, so I will concede on the low-weight issueD while asking for a moderate improvement on issueC.

The opponent has steadily conceded toward my preferred values, and their latest offer is already fairly strong for me, but we are still early at t=0.22 so I can seek a small additional gain above it. To avoid simply repeating my previous bid, I am accepting their concession on issueC while asking for a modest improvement on my lowest-weight issueD.

The opponent has conceded strongly on issueA and issueB, but is still pushing low values on issueC/issueD; their latest valid offer gives me about 0.775 utility. At t=0.24 I can still target above 0.80 while making a plausible tradeoff on the lower-weight issues, avoiding an exact repeat of my previous bid. I will hold firm on the high-weight issueA and issueB, concede issueC to valueE, and ask for a moderate improvement on issueD.

At t=0.26 I am still targeting a strong utility around 0.82 rather than accepting the opponent’s last 0.79 offer outright. The opponent has steadily conceded toward my preferred values on issueA and issueB, but is still holding harder on issueC/issueD; since issueD is lowest weight for me, I concede it fully while asking for a modest improvement on issueC.

At t=0.28 I am still targeting a fairly strong utility around 0.82 rather than immediately accepting the opponent’s lower-utility recent offer. The opponent has conceded substantially on issueA and mostly on issueB, while still pushing harder on issueC/issueD, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while making movement on lower-weight issueC and varying issueD to avoid repeating my prior bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.824, which is a modest improvement over the opponent’s latest offer to me while still conceding on the low-weight issueD. At t=0.30 there is still time, and the opponent has been steadily conceding from very poor offers toward my preferred values, so I will hold firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while asking for a small improvement on issueC.

I am targeting a utility around 0.80, conceding moderately because we are still only at t=0.32 and not yet under severe deadline pressure. The opponent has improved substantially from all valueF to offers with issueA/valueA and issueB near valueA, but they are holding issueC/issueD low; I will concede on lower-weight issueC and issueD while keeping my high-weight issueA and issueB at valueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.82, keeping my highest-weight issues issueA and issueB at valueA while conceding on lower-weight issueC/issueD. At t=0.34 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest A/A/E/F outright; their offers have improved to about 0.79 for me but are now oscillating, so I will make a slightly better-for-me counter that is still close to their signaled range.
